title: Following the Equator: A Journey Around the World
author: Mark Twain
evidence_summary: Useful for the persona's social stance: Twain watches empire, missionary language, and racial hierarchy with sardonic distance, often exposing cruelty by describing it plainly and then twisting the knife with irony.
notes: Public-domain source suitable for open-source citation. Helps ground the persona's anti-pomp, anti-hypocrisy commentary and travel-observer mode.
